How to Never Be Late to Meetings

Being late to meetings is usually not a character flaw. It is a systems problem. People run late because transitions are easy to miss, schedules get packed, reminders are too passive, and travel time gets underestimated.

Download on the App Store

Why people are late to meetings

People are often late for predictable reasons:

  • •they underestimate how long it takes to wrap up the current task
  • •they rely on a single quiet notification
  • •they have back-to-back meetings with no transition buffer
  • •they underestimate travel time
  • •they struggle with time blindness or inconsistent routines

Why normal calendar alerts often fail

Most calendar apps send passive notifications. They are useful, but they are easy to swipe away, easy to ignore, and easy to forget five minutes later. That is why many people see the reminder and still arrive late.

What actually helps

A better system usually includes:

  • ✓more than one reminder
  • ✓stronger transition cues
  • ✓enough buffer before the meeting
  • ✓a leave-time signal for in-person events
  • ✓an alarm that is harder to ignore than a normal notification

How OnTimer helps

OnTimer connects to your calendar and turns upcoming meetings and events into loud, persistent alarms. Instead of relying only on a passive calendar notification, you get a stronger signal before it is too late.

For events with a location, Time To Leave can alert you when it is time to leave based on travel time and traffic. Time To Leave is a paid feature.

OnTimer also supports multiple calendars, which is helpful if your real schedule lives across more than one account.

See all features →FAQ →

FAQ

Why do I keep being late to meetings?

Usually because the reminder system is too weak. Quiet notifications, packed schedules, underestimated prep time, and missed transitions all contribute.

How can I get better reminders for meetings on iPhone?

Use a system that gives you a stronger signal than a standard calendar notification. That can mean multiple reminders, more lead time, or an app that creates louder, more persistent alarms.

Is there an app that gives loud alarms for calendar events?

Yes. OnTimer is designed to trigger loud, persistent alarms before meetings and calendar events.

How do Time To Leave alerts work?

For events with a location, Time To Leave estimates when you should head out based on travel time and traffic. It is a paid feature in OnTimer.

Can I use OnTimer with more than one calendar?

Yes. OnTimer supports multiple calendars.
